chosen_evolution_point.selection_rationale: Previous iteration delivered a small positive area gain from exact area optimization with unchanged delay. With Area Priority and no delay stagnation, tightening phase-dropping decisions to avoid area increases is a low-risk lever. Among modules: match_phase.cpp affects early cost but may require broader rebalancing; match_phase_exact.cpp was just tuned and may face diminishing returns; match_drop_phase.cpp governs the final single-phase vs dual-phase choice and inverter accounting, where decisions currently consider only timing in DO_AREA mode. Adding an area-flow guard here directly prevents area regressions and can reduce total area without impacting timing. Diversity signals also recommend exploring match_drop_phase.cpp.

evolution_step.evolution_point_id: tech_map_impl::match_drop_phase (EVOLVE-BLOCK), DO_AREA branch: unify decision for use_zero/use_one
evolution_step.objective: Prevent phase unification when it does not reduce area_flow compared to keeping both phases, while respecting required time; thereby trimming area in the final decision stage.
evolution_step.constraints: Modify only within EVOLVE-BLOCK; preserve templates/APIs; keep existing epsilon-based compares; do not alter cut generation or broader control flow; ensure required time checks remain unchanged.
evolution_step.rationale: In DO_AREA mode, match_drop_phase currently unifies phases solely on timing feasibility, which can inadvertently increase area due to inverter overhead versus dual-phase coverage. By adding an area-flow guard against the combined cost of both phases, we only apply unification when it lowers area_flow. Required time constraints remain intact, preserving delay. This change targets end-of-flow cleanups, complementing last iteration’s exact-area tuning, and is a single, bounded decision-point modification aligned with Area Priority.
